question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 6.00% on 2026-04-24 for the Metaculus question "Will the United States gain formal sovereignty over any part of Greenland during 2026?"?
summary: Meta-prediction on whether Metaculus CP for US-Greenland sovereignty will be strictly above 6.00% on April 24. CP is currently at exactly 6.00% (the threshold), which creates genuine uncertainty. However, the strong historical mean-reversion pattern (CP has oscillated between 4-6%, always reverting after spikes), the fact that 6% is the all-time high (likely a local peak), and the extremely low probability of the underlying event all favor the CP falling back below the threshold. Partially offset by a weak upward trend and ongoing news flow. Monte Carlo models range from 23% to 40% depending on mean-reversion strength, with the mean-reverting model most consistent with observed data.

factors.description: CP is exactly at the 6.00% threshold - must be STRICTLY above to resolve YES. Being exactly at the line means any tiny fluctuation downward resolves NO, creating asymmetric risk.
Strong historical mean-reversion pattern: CP oscillated 4→4.9→4→5→4→6, with every upward spike followed by return to ~4%. The 6% is the all-time high and likely a local peak.
Mild upward linear trend over 6 weeks (+0.24%/week) suggests gradual drift higher, partially offsetting mean reversion.

Underlying event extremely unlikely: Greenland PM rejects sovereignty transfer, Denmark classified US as security threat, 85% of Greenlanders oppose, Trump reversed at Davos. Fundamentals don't support CP rising further.
Ongoing Trump rhetoric and NATO trilateral discussions keep the topic alive, could attract new forecasters who push estimates slightly higher.

Manifold Markets at 7% for US controlling Greenland by EOY2026 - roughly in line with current Metaculus CP, suggesting 6% is near cross-platform equilibrium rather than significantly below fair value.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 35% probability (YES)**

The CP is sitting exactly at the 6.00% threshold, creating a knife-edge situation. My analysis leans toward NO (65%) based on:

- **Mean reversion dominates**: Every prior upward CP spike was followed by a return to ~4%. The current 6% is the all-time high and likely a peak.
- **Strict inequality**: Even staying at 6.00% exactly resolves NO — the bar is asymmetrically tight.
- **No catalysts expected**: The underlying Greenland sovereignty question is overwhelmingly likely to resolve NO. Without new dramatic developments, forecasters have little reason to push estimates higher.
- **Cross-platform consistency**: Manifold at ~7% suggests the current CP level is near fair value, not below it.

The 35% for YES reflects genuine uncertainty from the short time window, weak upward trend, and the possibility that whatever pushed CP from 4% to 6% could persist.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41502) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-24 23:09:42 is higher than 6.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 6.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-24 23:09:42, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
